checks.observed: Arabic routes rendered with lang=ar and dir=rtl in an authenticated QA session.
Arabic More and dashboard chrome rendered localized Arabic labels and counters without Hebrew leakage.
Vehicle question 41 and all four options rendered in Arabic with no Hebrew characters in main content.
Flashcard 1 of 277 rendered the Arabic sign name and Arabic controls.
More rendered in Arabic after the locale switch and exposed the return-to-Hebrew control.

checks.reason: Reload persistence was observed, but bare-root redirect behavior was not rechecked in the Arabic session.

checks.observed: Arabic More rendered localized counters and the Arabic-Indic date ١٥ يوليو without RTL breakage.
A React console error occurs on every observed Hebrew-to-Arabic client locale switch.
Arabic pages exposed headings, named buttons and links, localized image alt text, and lang/dir metadata.
Observed Arabic quiz, flashcard, and More copy contained no Hebrew leakage or direction breakage.

findings.title: Avoid React script-tag console error when switching locales
findings.expected: Changing locale should complete without an error-level React console message.
findings.actual: React logs that a script tag was encountered while rendering a component; it reproduced twice.

not_tested: Bare-root Arabic redirect after locale persistence.
Arabic exam, schedule, and review/retry flows.
limitations: QA ran against next dev; the finding is a browser console error, while the rendered Arabic UI remained usable.
